title: corylus rostrata var. mandshurica
intro: Corylus rostrata var. mandshurica, also known as the Manchurian filbert or Manchurian hazelnut, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northeastern Asia, characterized by its edible nuts and leaves. The plant's leaves and nuts have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Corylus rostrata var. mandshurica include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves and nuts can also be used in cooking and as a natural remedy for various ailments.
